UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile, containing personal information and references to their preferences and movie personality.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

username.description: A unique display name chosen by the user.

avatarUrl.description: URL to the user's profile avatar image.

dateJoined.description: The date and time when the user registered.

moviePersonalityTypeId.description: Reference to the MoviePersonalityType assigned to the user after taking the quiz. (Relationship: MoviePersonalityType 1:N UserProfile)

favoriteGenreIds.description: References to Genres that the user considers favorites. (Relationship: Genre N:N UserProfile)

favoriteActorIds.description: References to Persons (actors) that the user considers favorites. (Relationship: Person N:N UserProfile)

favoriteDirectorIds.description: References to Persons (directors) that the user considers favorites. (Relationship: Person N:N UserProfile)

Movie.title: Movie
Movie.description: Represents a movie, storing its details primarily sourced from TMDB and used across user watchlists and recommendations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Movie entity.

tmdbId.description: The unique identifier for the movie from The Movie Database (TMDB).

title.description: The official title of the movie.

overview.description: A brief summary or plot overview of the movie.

releaseDate.description: The official release date of the movie.

posterUrl.description: URL to the movie's poster image.

backdropUrl.description: URL to the movie's backdrop image, suitable for background visuals.

averageRating.description: The average rating of the movie (e.g., from TMDB or aggregated from user ratings).

runtime.description: The duration of the movie in minutes.

trailerUrl.description: URL to the movie's official trailer.

genreIds.description: References to Genres associated with this movie. (Relationship: Genre N:N Movie)

castMemberIds.description: References to Persons (actors) who are part of the movie's cast. (Relationship: Person N:N Movie)

directorIds.description: References to Persons (directors) who directed the movie. (Relationship: Person N:N Movie)

Genre.title: Genre
Genre.description: Represents a movie genre, used for categorization and filtering.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Genre entity.

tmdbId.description: The unique identifier for the genre from The Movie Database (TMDB).

Person.title: Person
Person.description: Represents an individual involved in movies, such as an actor or director.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Person entity.

tmdbId.description: The unique identifier for the person from The Movie Database (TMDB).

name.description: The full name of the person.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the person's profile image.

WatchlistEntry.title: WatchlistEntry
WatchlistEntry.description: Represents a movie in a user's watchlist, including its status (watched/unwatched), personal rating, and notes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WatchlistEntry entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this watchlist entry. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N WatchlistEntry)

movieId.description: Reference to the Movie associated with this watchlist entry. (Relationship: Movie 1:N WatchlistEntry)

addedDate.description: The date and time when the movie was added to the watchlist.

isWatched.description: Indicates whether the user has marked the movie as watched.

watchDate.description: The date and time when the user watched the movie (if 'isWatched' is true).

personalRating.description: The user's personal rating for the movie (e.g., 1-10).

notes.description: Personal notes or a review written by the user about the movie.

rewatchCount.description: The number of times the user has rewatched this movie.

Friendship.title: Friendship
Friendship.description: Represents a friendship connection between two users on the platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Friendship entity.

requesterId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who initiated the friend request. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Friendship)

addresseeId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who received the friend request. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Friendship)

status.description: The current status of the friendship request (e.g., 'pending', 'accepted', 'declined').

establishedDate.description: The date and time when the friendship was established (accepted).

MoviePersonalityType.title: MoviePersonalityType
MoviePersonalityType.description: Defines a cinema personality type that users can be classified into based on their movie preferences.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MoviePersonalityType entity.

name.description: The name of the personality type (e.g., 'Action Addict', 'Sci-Fi Thinker').

description.description: A detailed description of the characteristics of this personality type.

Comment.title: Comment
Comment.description: Represents a comment made by a user on a friend's movie review or watchlist entry.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Comment entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who authored the comment. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Comment)

watchlistEntryId.description: Reference to the WatchlistEntry that this comment is associated with. (Relationship: WatchlistEntry 1:N Comment)

text.description: The actual content of the comment.

commentDate.description: The date and time when the comment was posted.

parentCommentId.description: Optional reference to a parent comment, allowing for threaded discussions. (Relationship: Comment 1:N Comment)

Reaction.title: Reaction
Reaction.description: Represents a reaction (e.g., like, love) by a user to a comment or review.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Reaction entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who made the reaction. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Reaction)

commentId.description: Reference to the Comment that this reaction is associated with. (Relationship: Comment 1:N Reaction)

reactionDate.description: The date and time when the reaction was made.

definition.description: Stores individual user profile information. Includes denormalized 'friends' map for efficient authorization checks for social features. Accessible by the user and their accepted friends.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user (Firebase Authentication UID).

definition.description: Contains a user's specific movie entries, including watched status, personal ratings, and notes. Owned by the parent userId, and accessible by the user and their accepted friends.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns this watchlist entry.

params.name: watchlistEntryId
params.description: The unique ID of the watchlist entry.

definition.description: Stores cached movie details from TMDB. Publicly readable by all authenticated users. Write access restricted to administrators/backend processes.

params.name: movieId
params.description: The unique ID of the movie.

definition.description: Defines movie genres. Publicly readable by all authenticated users. Write access restricted to administrators/backend processes.

params.name: genreId
params.description: The unique ID of the genre.

definition.description: Stores details for movie cast and crew members (e.g., actors, directors). Publicly readable by all authenticated users. Write access restricted to administrators/backend processes.

params.name: personId
params.description: The unique ID of the person.

definition.description: Defines the various movie personality types. Publicly readable by all authenticated users. Write access restricted to administrators/backend processes.

params.name: typeId
params.description: The unique ID of the movie personality type.

definition.description: Manages friendship requests and their status between two users. Includes 'requesterId' and 'addresseeId' for authorization. Changes to friendship status trigger updates to denormalized 'friends' maps in UserProfile documents.

params.name: friendshipId
params.description: The unique ID of the friendship entry.

definition.description: Stores comments made by users on friends' movie reviews/watchlist entries. Includes denormalized 'userId' (author) and 'watchlistEntryOwnerId' for authorization independence. Readable by the author, the watchlist owner, and their respective friends.

params.name: commentId
params.description: The unique ID of the comment.

definition.description: Stores reactions to comments. Includes denormalized 'userId' (reactor), 'commentAuthorId', and 'watchlistEntryOwnerId' for authorization independence. Readable by the reactor, the comment author, the watchlist owner, and their respective friends.

params.name: reactionId
params.description: The unique ID of the reaction.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Access Modeling to ensure robust security rules and efficient queries. 

**Authorization Independence:**
1.  **User-specific data:** For highly private user data like `UserProfile` and `WatchlistEntry`, a hierarchical path `users/{userId}/watchlist/{watchlistEntryId}` is used. This embeds the `userId` directly into the path, allowing security rules to instantly verify `request.auth.uid == userId` without needing any `get()` operations on parent documents.
2.  **Collaborative Data (Friends):** Instead of relying on `get()` calls to a separate `friendships` collection for every friend access check, the `UserProfile` document denormalizes a `friends` map (`friends: { [friendUid]: true }`). This enables rules like `request.auth.uid in get(/users/userId).data.friends` for efficient read access to friends' profiles and watchlist entries without costly collection scans or multiple `get()` calls per access. The `friendships` collection still exists to manage friend request lifecycle and transactional updates to these denormalized `friends` maps (typically via Cloud Functions).
3.  **Nested Social Data (Comments/Reactions):** For `Comment` and `Reaction` documents, which are typically created by one user on another user's content (a `WatchlistEntry`), critical authorization IDs are denormalized into the document itself. A `Comment` includes its `authorUserId` (the commenter) and `watchlistEntryOwnerId` (the user who owns the `WatchlistEntry` being commented on). Similarly, `Reaction` documents contain `reactorUserId`, `commentAuthorId`, and `watchlistEntryOwnerId`. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls to the parent `WatchlistEntry` or `Comment` documents in security rules, ensuring atomic operations and simplified rule logic for social interactions.

**QAPs (Queries as Permissions) and Structural Segregation:**
1.  **User-Owned Data:** The path `users/{userId}/watchlist/{watchlistEntryId}` naturally supports QAPs. A user can query their own watchlist with `db.collection('users').doc(myUid).collection('watchlist')` and security rules will enforce `userId == request.auth.uid`. For friends, a similar query to `db.collection('users').doc(friendUid).collection('watchlist')` is secured by the denormalized `friends` map in the `UserProfile`.
2.  **Global Read-Only Data:** `movies`, `genres`, `persons`, and `moviePersonalityTypes` are segregated into top-level collections. These collections have a homogeneous security posture: publicly readable by any authenticated user and writeable only by an administrator or a trusted backend process. This simplifies rules for these reference data types.
3.  **Shared Social Interactions:** `comments` and `reactions` are top-level collections. Their denormalized authorization fields (`authorUserId`, `watchlistEntryOwnerId`, `reactorUserId`, `commentAuthorId`) enable QAPs for various social feeds. For example, a user can query for 'comments on my reviews' (`comments.where('watchlistEntryOwnerId', '==', request.auth.uid)`) or 'comments by my friends' (requiring client-side logic to combine `friends` list with `comment.authorUserId` or server-side filtering). The rules will check authorization against these denormalized fields.

This design prioritizes explicit authorization logic within each document or its immediate path, minimizing complex cross-document `get()` calls in security rules, which leads to more performant, debuggable, and secure Firestore. The denormalization of friendship status into the `UserProfile` further optimizes social feature permissions.
